content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and MMM.

Let pr(x)\text{pr}(x)pr(x) denote the number of distinct **prime** factors of xxx.

Count the number of integer arrays AAA such that:


AAA has length NNN.
1‚â§Ai‚â§M1 \leq A_i \leq M1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§M, and all the elements of AAA are **distinct**.
pr(Ai)‚â§pr(Aj)\text{pr}(A_i) \leq \text{pr}(A_j)pr(Ai‚Äã)‚â§pr(Aj‚Äã) for 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1‚â§i<j‚â§N.
The number of such arrays can be large, so print the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and the only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers - NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of arrays satisfying the conditions, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of MMM across all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105.
